summary: On March 12, 2026, IBM introduced the industry’s first published reference architecture for quantum-centric supercomputing, aiming to integrate quantum and classical computing through coordinated workflows and open software frameworks like Qiskit. This architecture allows quantum processors to work alongside classical CPUs and graphics processing units and has enabled practical scientific advances. Notable results include the creation and verification of a half-Möbius molecule (with collaborators including Oxford University and ETH Zurich), the simulation of a 303-atom protein at Cleveland Clinic, and large quantum simulations with RIKEN’s Fugaku supercomputer. These achievements demonstrate the capability of IBM’s framework to address complex scientific problems previously inaccessible to classical supercomputers alone.
